title: vector-param AD op + LOCAL-param capture fails LLVM verification: 'PtrToInt source must be pointer' (ptrtoint %eshkol_tagged_value)
goal: (define (mk a) (gradient (lambda (v) (* a (vref v 0) (vref v 0))) (vector 1.3 -0.7))) (mk 1.7) fails module verification on BOTH -r and AOT with 'PtrToInt source must be pointer: %N = ptrtoint %eshkol_tagged_value %a to i64'. Applies to ALL vector-param AD operators (gradient jacobian hessian divergence curl laplacian) and to vector captures used via vector-ref; scalar (derivative ...) with the identical local capture works, and capturing a GLOBAL works. Found by the AD composition oracle (P3): cells *.caplocal / *.capvrefout.
fix_hint: The vector-AD closure-capture path packs the captured function parameter (an eshkol_tagged_value STRUCT value) where a pointer is expected: somewhere in the AD lambda capture materialization a CreatePtrToInt is applied to the raw tagged-value aggregate instead of extracting the data field (ExtractValue idx 4) or spilling to an alloca and passing the address. Compare with how codegenClosureCall/derivative's scalar path passes the same capture.
acceptance: scripts/run_ad_oracle.sh: all 12 ad_oracle_xc_ESH-0097_* files flip XKNOWN->PASS
campaign_payoff: Any parameterized model factory (make-loss hyperparam) currently cannot take gradients of vector functions — this is the natural way users write training code.
